title: Catabrosa hydrophila
taxonomy: Catabrosa hydrophila belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a species of grass that is commonly found in wet environments. Catabrosa hydrophila is classified as a perennial plant, typically growing in saturated soil or shallow water. Within the family Poaceae, Catabrosa hydrophila is part of the subfamily Pooideae. This classification helps identify the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Catabrosa aquatica, also known as Catabrosa hydrophila, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of Catabrosa aquatica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Catabrosa aquatica include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
